breaches.title: Oracle PeopleSoft Global Zero-Day Campaign
breaches.tldr: The ShinyHunters group exploited a zero-day vulnerability in Oracle PeopleSoft to target over 100 organizations, including major universities and infrastructure firms. The campaign resulted in the theft of sensitive personal and corporate data globally.
breaches.action: Audit and patch all Oracle PeopleSoft installations immediately and monitor for unauthorized database access or data exfiltration.

breaches.title: Brightspeed Telecommunications Data Breach
breaches.tldr: A threat group known as 'Crimson Collective' successfully breached Brightspeed, impacting more than one million customers. Stolen data reportedly includes personal identification and service records.
breaches.action: Notify affected customers and implement enhanced multi-factor authentication (MFA) across all customer-facing portals.

breaches.title: Arch Linux AUR Supply Chain Compromise
breaches.tldr: Over 400 Arch Linux User Repository (AUR) packages were hijacked to distribute infostealers and eBPF-based rootkits. The malicious updates targeted developers and system administrators to gain high-level system persistence.
breaches.action: Verify the integrity of all AUR packages and perform a full system audit for any unrecognized eBPF programs or suspicious network activity.

breaches.title: American Tower Corporation Data Leak
breaches.tldr: ShinyHunters claimed responsibility for a significant data breach at American Tower Corporation, a major wireless infrastructure provider. The group published samples of internal documents and employee data as proof of the breach.
breaches.action: Perform a comprehensive audit of internal cloud storage permissions and reset all administrative credentials.

ransomware.title: The Gentlemen Ransomware AI-Driven Expansion
ransomware.tldr: A Qilin splinter group called 'The Gentlemen' has hit 478 victims across 66 countries using AI-assisted exploit development. They are offering insiders an unprecedented 90% share of ransom profits to secure initial access.
ransomware.action: Increase monitoring for insider threats and implement strict least-privilege access controls for all critical systems.

ransomware.title: Zayo Group Targeted by ShinyHunters
ransomware.tldr: Telecommunications giant Zayo Group was listed as a victim of a ransomware-style extortion attack by the ShinyHunters group. The attackers claim to have encrypted and exfiltrated terabytes of sensitive infrastructure data.
ransomware.action: Verify offline backup integrity and ensure all network segments are isolated to prevent lateral movement.

ransomware.title: Madison Square Garden Sports Corp Extortion
ransomware.tldr: MSG Sports was targeted in a cyberattack resulting in the encryption of internal systems and threats of data publication. The incident has reportedly disrupted administrative operations and internal communications.
ransomware.action: Deploy advanced endpoint detection and response (EDR) tools to identify and block ransomware execution patterns.

ransomware.title: DragonForce Attack on Astec Valves & Fittings
ransomware.tldr: The DragonForce ransomware group successfully targeted the Indian manufacturer Astec Valves & Fittings, exfiltrating critical engineering designs. The group is threatening to release the proprietary data unless a ransom is paid.
ransomware.action: Ensure all engineering and proprietary data is encrypted at rest and monitor for large-scale data transfers to unauthorized external IPs.

vulns.title: Critical Windows Kernel RCE (CVE-2026-45657)
vulns.tldr: A critical vulnerability in the Windows Kernel with a CVSS score of 9.8 allows for unauthenticated remote code execution. This flaw is being actively targeted in the wild to gain full system control.
vulns.action: Deploy the latest Windows security updates immediately to all workstations and servers.

vulns.title: Splunk Enterprise Unauthenticated RCE
vulns.tldr: A critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-20253) in Splunk Enterprise allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code with system privileges. The exploit leverages a flaw in the management port's handling of specific requests.
vulns.action: Update Splunk Enterprise instances to the latest version and restrict access to the management port (8089) via firewall rules.

vulns.title: HTTP.sys 'HTTP/2 Bomb' Denial of Service
vulns.tldr: A publicly disclosed vulnerability in Windows HTTP.sys allows attackers to crash web servers with minimal data packets. Known as the 'HTTP/2 Bomb,' it exploits the way the server handles oversized header frames.
vulns.action: Apply the June 2026 Patch Tuesday updates and consider disabling HTTP/2 on critical legacy servers if patching is delayed.

vulns.title: BitLocker Security Bypass via Bitskrieg
vulns.tldr: The 'Bitskrieg' vulnerability allows an attacker with physical access to bypass BitLocker full-disk encryption on Windows devices. The flaw exploits a timing issue during the pre-boot authentication phase.
vulns.action: Enable TPM+PIN protectors for BitLocker and update Windows to the latest version to mitigate this bypass.
